questions.questionText: The most common poison used for suicide in India is:
questions.explanation: Organophosphorus compounds (OP): most common suicidal poison in India (agrarian country, easy availability of insecticides). Mechanism: irreversible AChE inhibition → cholinergic crisis (DUMBBBELS). Intermediate syndrome: 24-96 hours, respiratory muscle paralysis. Treatment: (1) Decontamination, (2) Atropine (blocks muscarinic effects — titrate to drying of secretions), (3) Pralidoxime/PAM (reactivates AChE before aging — give within 24-48 hrs). Garlic-like odor. Miosis: most reliable sign. Reference: Reddy — OP Poisoning.

questions.optionB: Histotoxic hypoxia (CN- binds cytochrome oxidase a3 → blocks mitochondrial electron transport chain → cells cannot utilize oxygen)
questions.explanation: Cyanide: rapidly fatal poison. Mechanism: CN- binds cytochrome oxidase a3 (complex IV) in mitochondria → blocks ETC → cells cannot utilize O2 (histotoxic/cytotoxic hypoxia). Blood is oxygenated but cells can't use it → cherry-red postmortem lividity, venous blood appears bright red. Bitter almonds odor. Sources: HCN (industrial), potassium/sodium cyanide, amygdalin (cassava, bitter almonds, cherry pits). Antidotes: hydroxocobalamin (first line), sodium thiosulfate, amyl nitrite + sodium nitrite (Lilly kit — forms methemoglobin which binds CN). Reference: Reddy — Cyanide.

questions.questionText: Arsenic poisoning can be detected by:
questions.optionB: Reinsch test and Marsh test (on hair, nails, bones — arsenic persists long after death)
questions.explanation: Arsenic: 'King of poisons / Poison of kings.' Chronic: lines of Mees (white transverse lines on nails), raindrop pigmentation (hyperpigmentation with depigmented spots), keratosis, peripheral neuropathy, hepatotoxicity. Acute: rice-water stools (like cholera), garlic smell. Tests: Reinsch test (copper strip turns black), Marsh test (arsenic mirror — confirmatory). Sample: hair, nails, bones (arsenic deposits and persists). Arsenic in hair can determine timing of poisoning (grows ~1cm/month). Antidote: BAL (dimercaprol). Reference: Reddy — Arsenic.

questions.optionB: Anticholinergic toxidrome — mydriasis, dry mouth, tachycardia, urinary retention, delirium, hallucinations ('mad as a hatter')
questions.explanation: Datura (dhatura/thorn apple): contains tropane alkaloids (atropine, scopolamine, hyoscyamine). Anticholinergic toxidrome: 'Hot as a hare (hyperthermia), Blind as a bat (mydriasis/cycloplegia), Dry as a bone (no secretions), Red as a beet (flushed skin), Mad as a hatter (delirium, hallucinations), Full as a flask (urinary retention).' Used as: stupefying agent (roadside poisoning by criminals — added to food/drink). Seeds: most toxic part. Antidote: physostigmine (reversible AChE inhibitor that crosses BBB). Reference: Reddy — Datura.

questions.questionText: Carbon monoxide poisoning causes death by:
questions.optionB: CO binds hemoglobin with 200-250x greater affinity than O2 → forms carboxyhemoglobin (COHb) → prevents O2 transport → cherry-red color
questions.explanation: CO: colorless, odorless gas from incomplete combustion. Binds Hb with 200-250x affinity of O2 → COHb (cannot carry O2). Also: shifts O2-Hb dissociation curve LEFT (remaining O2 bound more tightly). Sources: automobile exhaust, house fires, coal burning (tandoor deaths), faulty heaters. Cherry-red/pink color of skin, blood, PM lividity (pathognomonic). Symptoms: headache, dizziness → confusion → coma → death. COHb >60%: fatal. Treatment: 100% O2 (displaces CO), hyperbaric O2 (severe). Reference: Reddy — CO Poisoning.
